---
title: "IBD Imaging Digest"
description: "An automated daily literature monitor for IBD imaging research. Fetches new papers, ranks by semantic relevance against a personal corpus, and delivers a tiered digest into Obsidian."
date: 2026-05-20
categories: [tooling, NLP, literature, obsidian]
---

## What it does

Keeping up with IBD imaging literature is a daily task that, left unstructured, turns into an hour of tab-opening and half-read abstracts. This pipeline replaces that with a ranked digest that arrives every morning in my Obsidian vault.

The system pulls from four sources: PubMed via E-utilities and three radiology journals (Radiology, Radiology: AI, European Radiology) via Crossref. Papers are deduplicated by DOI and stored in a local SQLite database. Each new paper is embedded with SPECTER2 and compared by cosine similarity to a seed corpus of papers I have curated in Zotero over the past few years. The resulting similarity scores are converted to percentile ranks and used to assign one of three tiers: Must-read (≥ 0.958), Skim (0.924 to 0.958), or Archive (below that).

The daily digest renders as an Obsidian Markdown file with callout blocks and checkbox conventions. The checkboxes are not cosmetic: they are intended to feed a future ranking improvement loop (Step 6) once there is enough signal from real use.

```{=html}
<svg width="100%" viewBox="0 0 680 360" role="img" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<title>IBD Imaging Digest pipeline</title>
<desc>Five-stage pipeline: fetch from four sources, deduplicate by DOI, rank with SPECTER2 against Zotero corpus, tier into must-read/skim/archive, render Obsidian digest.</desc>
<defs>
  <marker id="arrow" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto-start-reverse">
    <path d="M2 1L8 5L2 9" fill="none" stroke="context-stroke" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/>
  </marker>
  <style>
    .t  { font: 400 14px var(--bs-font-sans-serif, sans-serif); fill: var(--bs-body-color, #212529); }
    .ts { font: 400 12px var(--bs-font-sans-serif, sans-serif); fill: var(--bs-secondary-color, #6c757d); }
    .th { font: 500 14px var(--bs-font-sans-serif, sans-serif); fill: var(--bs-body-color, #212529); }
    .src  rect { fill: #e8f0eb; stroke: #5a7260; }
    .src  text { fill: #2c3d30; }
    .sem  rect { fill: #eeeef8; stroke: #6970a0; }
    .sem  text { fill: #2e2f5a; }
    .neu  rect { fill: #f0f0ee; stroke: #8a8a85; }
    .neu  text { fill: #3a3a38; }
    .must rect { fill: #fdf3e0; stroke: #b07a18; }
    .must text { fill: #4a3200; }
    .arch rect { fill: #faeae8; stroke: #b05040; }
    .arch text { fill: #4a1a14; }
    .badge-src  rect { fill: #e8f0eb; stroke: #5a7260; }
    .badge-src  text { fill: #2c3d30; }
    .badge-neu  rect { fill: #f0f0ee; stroke: #8a8a85; }
    .badge-neu  text { fill: #3a3a38; }
    @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
      .t  { fill: #d8d8d4; }
      .ts { fill: #9a9a94; }
      .th { fill: #d8d8d4; }
      .src  rect { fill: #1e2e22; stroke: #5a7260; }
      .src  text { fill: #a8c4ac; }
      .sem  rect { fill: #1e1e30; stroke: #6970a0; }
      .sem  text { fill: #b0b4d8; }
      .neu  rect { fill: #242420; stroke: #6a6a64; }
      .neu  text { fill: #b0b0a8; }
      .must rect { fill: #2a2010; stroke: #b07a18; }
      .must text { fill: #e0c070; }
      .arch rect { fill: #2a1510; stroke: #b05040; }
      .arch text { fill: #e0a090; }
      .badge-src  rect { fill: #1e2e22; stroke: #5a7260; }
      .badge-src  text { fill: #a8c4ac; }
      .badge-neu  rect { fill: #242420; stroke: #6a6a64; }
      .badge-neu  text { fill: #b0b0a8; }
    }
  </style>
</defs>

<text class="ts" x="68"  y="18" text-anchor="middle">fetch</text>
<text class="ts" x="224" y="18" text-anchor="middle">deduplicate</text>
<text class="ts" x="368" y="18" text-anchor="middle">rank</text>
<text class="ts" x="510" y="18" text-anchor="middle">tier</text>
<text class="ts" x="632" y="18" text-anchor="middle">digest</text>

<g class="src"><rect x="10" y="30"  width="116" height="36" rx="4" stroke-width="0.5"/><text class="ts" x="68" y="52"  text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">PubMed (E-utilities)</text></g>
<g class="src"><rect x="10" y="76"  width="116" height="36" rx="4" stroke-width="0.5"/><text class="ts" x="68" y="98"  text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">Radiology</text></g>
<g class="src"><rect x="10" y="122" width="116" height="36" rx="4" stroke-width="0.5"/><text class="ts" x="68" y="144" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">Radiology: AI</text></g>
<g class="src"><rect x="10" y="168" width="116" height="36" rx="4" stroke-width="0.5"/><text class="ts" x="68" y="190" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">European Radiology</text></g>
<text class="ts" x="68" y="224" text-anchor="middle">via Crossref</text>

<line x1="126" y1="48"  x2="148" y2="110" stroke="#8a8a85" stroke-width="0.8" fill="none" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
<line x1="126" y1="94"  x2="148" y2="112" stroke="#8a8a85" stroke-width="0.8" fill="none" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
<line x1="126" y1="140" x2="148" y2="115" stroke="#8a8a85" stroke-width="0.8" fill="none" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
<line x1="126" y1="186" x2="148" y2="118" stroke="#8a8a85" stroke-width="0.8" fill="none" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>

<g class="neu"><rect x="150" y="82" width="130" height="72" rx="4" stroke-width="0.5"/><text class="th" x="215" y="108" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">DOI dedup</text><text class="ts" x="215" y="130" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">SQLite store</text></g>

<line x1="280" y1="118" x2="302" y2="118" stroke="#8a8a85" stroke-width="0.8" fill="none" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>

<g class="sem"><rect x="304" y="60" width="128" height="116" rx="4" stroke-width="0.5"/><text class="th" x="368" y="86"  text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">SPECTER2</text><text class="ts" x="368" y="106" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">cosine similarity</text><text class="ts" x="368" y="124" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">vs Zotero corpus</text><text class="ts" x="368" y="142" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">percentile rank</text><text class="ts" x="368" y="160" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">local cache</text></g>

<line x1="432" y1="118" x2="454" y2="118" stroke="#8a8a85" stroke-width="0.8" fill="none" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>

<g class="must"><rect x="456" y="60"  width="128" height="36" rx="4" stroke-width="0.5"/><text class="ts" x="520" y="82"  text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">Must-read  &#8805; 0.958</text></g>
<g class="neu" ><rect x="456" y="106" width="128" height="36" rx="4" stroke-width="0.5"/><text class="ts" x="520" y="128" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">Skim  0.924 &#8211; 0.958</text></g>
<g class="arch"><rect x="456" y="152" width="128" height="36" rx="4" stroke-width="0.5"/><text class="ts" x="520" y="174" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">Archive  &lt; 0.924</text></g>

<line x1="584" y1="78"  x2="606" y2="110" stroke="#8a8a85" stroke-width="0.8" fill="none" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
<line x1="584" y1="124" x2="606" y2="118" stroke="#8a8a85" stroke-width="0.8" fill="none" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
<line x1="584" y1="170" x2="606" y2="126" stroke="#8a8a85" stroke-width="0.8" fill="none" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>

<g class="sem"><rect x="594" y="82" width="76" height="72" rx="4" stroke-width="0.5"/><text class="th" x="632" y="106" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">Obsidian</text><text class="ts" x="632" y="126" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">callouts</text><text class="ts" x="632" y="146" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">checkboxes</text></g>

<path d="M632 154 Q632 240 510 252 Q388 264 368 196" fill="none" stroke="#6970a0" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="4 3" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
<text class="ts" x="480" y="272" text-anchor="middle">step 6: feedback loop (deferred)</text>

<g class="badge-src"><rect x="10"  y="296" width="108" height="26" rx="3" stroke-width="0.5"/><text class="ts" x="64"  y="313" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">GitHub Actions</text></g>
<g class="badge-src"><rect x="128" y="296" width="96"  height="26" rx="3" stroke-width="0.5"/><text class="ts" x="176" y="313" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">daily schedule</text></g>
<g class="badge-neu"><rect x="234" y="296" width="116" height="26" rx="3" stroke-width="0.5"/><text class="ts" x="292" y="313" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">SPECTER2 cached</text></g>

<text class="ts" x="10" y="348" text-anchor="start">deferred: LLM summaries &#183; citation counts &#183; Europe PMC &#183; bioRxiv &#183; notifications</text>
</svg>
```

## Why SPECTER2 and a personal corpus

Generic keyword search returns too much and misses context. SPECTER2 is trained on citation graphs, which means papers that are conceptually related to my seed set score higher even if they use different terminology. The Zotero corpus acts as a standing definition of what "relevant" means for my specific research questions: quantitative MRI in IBD, motility, Bayesian modeling, and clinical AI pipelines. The ranker inherits that definition automatically.

The percentile-based thresholds are a deliberate choice over fixed cosine cutoffs. Score distributions shift with topic and corpus size. Calibrating to percentiles keeps the tiers stable relative to each other, independent of those shifts.

## Infrastructure

The pipeline runs via GitHub Actions on a daily schedule. SPECTER2 is cached on the runner to avoid repeated downloads. The Obsidian vault doubles as the Git repository.

## Current state

Steps 1 through 5 are complete: fetching, deduplication, SPECTER2 ranking, Obsidian formatting, GitHub Actions scheduling, and threshold recalibration.

The current review workflow is intentionally manual. Each day I pull the repository, read the digest, and check off papers in Obsidian before pushing. That checkpoint is where the real filtering happens for now: the ranker proposes, the reading confirms or overrides. Step 6 will eventually parse those checkbox states to sharpen the ranker automatically, but that requires a few weeks of consistent signal before it is worth building.

Out of scope for v1: LLM abstract summaries, citation counts, Europe PMC / bioRxiv / medRxiv / arXiv, and Telegram or email notifications.

## What is next

A few weeks of daily use will tell me whether the tier thresholds are well calibrated or need adjustment. After that, Step 6 becomes the next meaningful milestone.

Longer term, I plan to publish a weekly IBD Digest on this site: a short roundup of the must-read papers from the previous seven days, with brief commentary on what is interesting and why. That serves two purposes. It forces me to actually read the digest rather than just generate it, and it creates a public record of what is worth paying attention to in this field.

---

*Built in one day using Python, SPECTER2, SQLite, and GitHub Actions, with Claude Code.*
